w:r.w:t: This document defines the role-based access control (RBAC) requirements for the
application. It specifies which pages/modules each role can access, what actions are
, and highlights known issues requiring resolution.
System-Level Role: System Admin — manages client
Management Roles: Company Admin— full access to all company modules.
_*): Manage inventory, schedules, work orders, and pipeline stages.
Quality Roles (QI_*): Handle quality inspection steps in pipeline and quality modules.
roles are defined across 3 layers: System, Management, and Operational (Production + Quality + Store).
a consolidated view of module-level access across all roles. ✔ = Access Granted, ❌ = No Access, ⚠ = Bug / Not Working.
Each sub-section below covers the page-level access and functional requirements for a specific role.
Super-level administrator responsible for managing client
within the platform. Has exclusive access to the Client Management System.
, plant name, email, password, (can add multiple plant, default 1 plant is
, machines.... related to this client should
of all registered clients with search &
No access to any company-internal modules
Highest-level administrator within a company instance. Has full access to all modules including Dashboard, Analytics, Inventory, Schedules, Work Orders, Pipeline, Quality, Reports, and Configuration.
Detailed History, Work Order Summary, Shift Report, Daily Report, Machine Analytics
Roles, Users, Shifts, Departments, Machine Type, Machines, Process, Reasons, Programs
Full access to Dashboard with all widgets and KPIs
Full analytics with export and filter capabilities
Configuration: manage all master data (Roles, Users, Shifts, Departments, Machine Types, Machines, Processes, Reasons, Programs)
. Limited access to Dashboard only (no Analytics). No access to scheduling, work orders, pipeline, quality, reports,
, he his responsible to add incoming raw material, and monthly schedule
QI based on that system admin
approve or reject, then only should updat
No access: Analytics, Schedules, Work Orders, Pipeline, Quality, Reports, Configuration
Dashboard: Read-only view with inventory-relevant KPIs
Fields: Item Name, Category, Quantity, Unit, Location, Status
No access to approval/rejection workflows (those are
No Analytics, Scheduling, Work Order, or Pipeline access
Dedicated quality inspector role for inventory. Can only
inventory items. No other module access.
checking the incoming stock, ok or not ok with proper reasons.
it includes client rejection, sub vender item received based on workorder.
Inventory, Dashboard, Analytics, Schedules, Work Orders, Pipeline, Quality, Reports, Configuration
Inventory page scoped to QI actions only
View list of pending inventory items awaiting QI
Approve action: mark item as approved with optional comments
Reject action: mark item as rejected with mandatory reason
No access to any other module
Production manager overseeing the full production lifecycle — Dashboard, Inventory, Schedules, Work Orders, and Pipeline. No Analytics, Quality approvals, Reports, or Configuration.
Detailed History, Work Order Summary, Shift Report, Daily Report, Machine Analytics
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.
Production technician for Die Casting department. Can
inventory, work orders, and pipeline stages but cannot Edit work orders or Verify quality.
The user can start the assigned work order by specifying the planned start date, end date, and assigning the machine, shift, and operator. Once the work order is started, it can only be paused.
During production planning, while mapping the part, the Die Casting Manager must select the program
to run on the specific machine. This program should be selected from the master list. In the mapping stage, the program should be available as a dropdown list.
The Die Casting PDC Manager will then choose the appropriate program for that particular production.
If a pause occurs during production, the Die Casting Manager must enter the reason for the pause.
If the work order is moved outside for production, the Die Casting PDC Manager should plan accordingly. If
for vendors, it should be arranged as discussed in
Once the work order is completed along with PDC quality checks, it must be
before being sent to the next stage. This process should be followed for each work order.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.
Detailed History, Work Order Summary, Shift Report, Daily Report, Machine Analytics
Quality Inspector for the Die Casting department. Responsible for Pipeline quality steps — Start QI and Approve/Reject quality outcomes.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process

The Die Casting QI will handle rejection and rework entries for parts, with a one-week review period. If the Die Casting QI has already raised a rejection and sent it for approval to the Die Casting PDC Manager, and the manager has approved it, then this quality record should not appear in the one-week review list.

The Die Casting QI will reject parts by providing a reason and will record any rework parts along with the reason. All details must be recorded against the work order, including the date, machine, shift during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details must be

If the production is carried out by a vendor, all details must also be recorded against the work order, including the vendor name, date, machine, shift during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details must be
No access: Analytics, Inventory, Schedules, Work
Start action scoped to QI-relevant stages
No Inventory, Schedule, Work Order, or Configuration access
feature exists but is currently non-functional (noted as 'Available but not working'). Manages work orders and
The user can start the assigned work order by specifying the planned start date, end date, and assigning the shift, and operator. Once the work order is started, it can only be paused.
During production planning, while mapping the part, the coating Manager must select the program
to run on the specific machine. This program should be selected from the master list. In the mapping stage, the program should be available as a dropdown list.
The coating PDC Manager will then choose the appropriate program for that particular production.
Note: If a pause occurs during production, the coating Manager must enter the reason for the pause.
If the work order is moved outside for production, the coating PDC Manager should plan accordingly. I
it should be arranged as discussed in
Once the work order is completed along with PDC quality checks, it must be
before being sent to the next stage. This process should be followed for each work order.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.
Detailed History, Work Order Summary, Shift Report, Daily Report
Quality Inspector for the Coating department. Similar to
Casting. Inventory QI actions available but not working.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.

Note: coating QI will handle the rejection and rework of entries for parts, allowing for a one-week review period. If the coating QI has already raised a rejection once and sent it for approval to the Die coating PDC Manager, and the manager has approved it, then this quality record should not appear in the one-week list.

The coating QI will reject the part with a reason and record any rework parts along with the reason. All details must be recorded against the work order, including the date, shift during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details should be
No access: Analytics, Inventory, Schedules, Work
Pipeline: View Start action scoped to QI-relevant stages
QI findings, approve quality checks, Reject and Rework with reasons
No Inventory, Schedule, Work Order, or Configuration access
Production technician for Machining department. Similar access to
The user can start the assigned work order by specifying the planned start date, end date, and assigning the machine, shift, and operator. Once the work order is started, it can only be paused.
During production planning, while mapping the part, the Machining PDC Manager must select the program
to run on the specific machine. This program should be selected from the master list. In the mapping stage, the program should be available as a dropdown list.
The Machining PDC Manager will then choose the appropriate program for that particular production.
Note: If a pause occurs during production, the Machining PDC Manager must enter the reason for the pause.
If the work order is moved outside for production, the Machining PDC Manager should plan accordingly. If
for vendors, it should be arranged as discussed in
Once the work order is completed along with PDC quality checks, it must be
before being sent to the next stage. This process should be followed for each work order.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.
Detailed History, Work Order Summary, Shift Report, Daily Report, Machine Analytics
, Work Order Edit, Pipeline QI/Verify, Quality, Reports, Configuration
Quality Inspector for the Machining department. Inventory QI actions available in UI but not working.
based on work order in which stage the workorder is currently and parts, and history of the process.

The Machining QI will handle rejection and rework entries for parts, with a one-week review period. If the Machining QI has already raised a rejection and sent it for approval to the Machining PDC Manager, and the manager has approved it, then this quality record should not appear in the one-week review list.

The Machining QI will reject parts by providing a reason and will record any rework parts along with the reason. All details must be recorded against the work order, including the date, machine, shift during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details must be

If the production is carried out by a vendor, all details must also be recorded against the work order, including the vendor name, date, machine, shift during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details must be
No access: Analytics, Inventory, Schedules, Work
Pipeline: View Start action scoped to QI-relevant stages
QI findings, approve quality checks, Reject and Rework with reasons
No Inventory, Schedule, Work Order, or Configuration access
Final Quality Inspector. Last line of quality control before output. Inventory QI
in UI but not working. Handles final Pipeline QI and Quality approvals.
Note: The Final QI will handle rejection and
entries for parts, with a one-week review period.

The Final QI will reject parts by providing a reason and will record any rework parts along with the reason. All details must be recorded against the work order, including the date during which production occurred, and the QI user ID of the person who rejected or marked the parts for rework. These details must be
No access: Analytics, Schedules, Work Orders, Reports, Configuration
The following roles have Inventory actions that are visible in the UI but not functioning. These require investigation and fixes before production release.
Add, Approve, Reject — visible in UI, not operational
Approve, Reject — visible in UI, not operational
Add, Approve, Reject — visible in UI, not operational
Add, Approve, Reject — visible in UI, not operational
Role enforcement must be applied at both the UI layer (hide/disable controls) and the API/backend layer.
Unauthorized access attempts must be logged and return a 403 Forbidden response.
Session tokens must carry role information and be
Role changes must take effect on next login or session refresh.
to users which actions are unavailable and why (permission-based messaging).
must be resolved before production deployment.
Production Technician / Production Team Lead — responsible for executing production tasks
Quality Inspector — responsible for quality verification and approval/rejection
The staged workflow from production start through quality verification to output/routing
A formal instruction to produce a specified quantity of product
a quality inspection report for a pipeline stage
Action to advance a pipeline item to the next processing stage after quality clearance
Role-Based Access Control — the permission model used in this application
